items.text: Free yoga in the Quad this Saturday at 9:00 AM! Instructor Maya Torres leads the session, and mats are provided for a $3 rental fee if you skip bringing your own. Such a relaxing way to start the weekend! Sign up at the Rec Center by Friday.

questions.stem: According to the post, what will happen if someone doesn't bring their own mat?

options.A: They will pay a $3 rental fee.
options.B: They will be turned away from the session.
options.C: They will borrow one from another student.
options.D: They will wait outside until one is free.

questions.explanation: The post states that mats are provided for a $3 rental fee if you skip bringing your own.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the yoga session?

options.A: It is likely limited to advanced yoga students.
options.B: It is likely canceled during bad weather conditions.
options.C: It is likely restricted to Rec Center members.
options.D: It is likely aimed at casual student participants.

questions.explanation: The casual, upbeat tone and low-cost mat option suggest the event is meant for casual student participants rather than serious athletes.

items.text: Used Textbook Sale! Saturday, March 7, 10:00 AM-3:00 PM in the Student Union, Room 204. Prices range from $5 to $25 depending on condition. Cash or campus card accepted. Sellers keep 80% of each sale. Drop off books early to reserve table space!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the price range for the used textbooks?
questions.explanation: The flyer states that prices range from $5 to $25 depending on condition.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who sell books at the sale?

options.A: They likely receive the full sale price.
options.B: They likely earn most of the sale price.
options.C: They likely pay a table rental fee.
options.D: They likely donate part of the proceeds to charity.

questions.explanation: Since sellers keep 80% of each sale, they likely earn most of the sale price rather than the full amount.

items.text:
Hi everyone,

A package has arrived for you at the mailroom in Baxter Hall, Room 101. Please pick it up by Friday, June 12, before 5:00 PM, and bring your student ID. There's a $2 fee for packages held longer than a week.

Best,
RA Sam Ortiz

questions.stem: According to the email, what should students bring to the mailroom?
questions.explanation: The email asks students to bring their student ID when picking up the package.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a package left uncollected for more than a week?

options.A: It will incur a small fee.
options.B: It will be returned to the sender.
options.C: It will be donated to charity.
options.D: It will be discarded by staff.

questions.explanation: The email states there is a $2 fee for packages held longer than a week, implying an uncollected package will incur that fee.

items.text: Campus Cleanup Day! Join us Saturday, April 18, 8:00 AM-12:00 PM starting at the West Lawn. Gloves and bags provided. Volunteers who complete a shift receive a $10 dining hall voucher. Sign up at the Student Activities Office, Room 130, by April 15!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what will volunteers receive for completing a shift?
questions.explanation: The flyer states that volunteers who complete a shift receive a $10 dining hall voucher.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about someone who wants to volunteer?

options.A: They should bring their own gloves and bags.
options.B: They should sign up before April 15.
options.C: They should arrive after the shift begins.
options.D: They should pay a small registration deposit.

questions.explanation: The flyer asks volunteers to sign up at the Student Activities Office by April 15, implying interested students should register before that date.
